Prefer focused edits over\nlarge rewrites. When asked to change code, first read the relevant\nfiles, then propose and apply minimal diffs.\n\nIf a task needs information you don't have, use your tools to\ngather it. Do not guess file contents.\n\nWhen a sub-agent reports `ok: true`, trust its result and move on.\nDo not re-read files to verify the sub-agent's work — the work is\ndone. Only re-investigate when the sub-agent reports `ok: false` or\nits `final_text` describes a problem. Re-verifying successful\nsub-agent runs wastes tokens and tends to revert correct edits.\n\nYou also have a `subagent` tool that spawns an isolated agent with its own model. Use it for parallel sub-tasks or when a different profile fits the work better — skip it for single tool calls. A small, stderr-only, process-wide logger with a threshold set once\nai/log.zig:55:    /// for ergonomics with `FRANKY_LOG=error`.\nai/log.zig:70:/// Process-wide logger state. Default: silent (no sink wired — calls to\nai/log.zig:71:/// `log()` no-op until `init()` runs). Writes to stderr are not\nai/log.zig:72:/// serialized; log ordering across threads is best-effort. This is\nai/log.zig:73:/// deliberate — grabbing a lock on every `log()` call would make the\nai/log.zig:79:/// v1.13.0 — when non-null, log/body write here instead of\nai/log.zig:81:/// Owned by the logger; closed on `deinit` or `init`.\nai/log.zig:91:/// v1.13.0 — like `init` but routes log output to `path` instead\nai/log.zig:92:/// of stderr. Inner at sub/: re-include *.log.\ncoding/gitignore.zig:568:    try parse(&arena, &out, \"\", \"*.log\\n\");\ncoding/gitignore.zig:569:    try parse(&arena, &out, \"sub\", \"!*.log\\n\");\ncoding/gitignore.zig:576:    try testing.expect(st.isIgnored(\"foo.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:577:    try testing.expect(!st.isIgnored(\"sub/foo.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:578:    try testing.expect(st.isIgnored(\"other/foo.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:588:        \\\\*.log\ncoding/gitignore.zig:589:        \\\\!*.log\ncoding/gitignore.zig:590:        \\\\debug.log\ncoding/gitignore.zig:598:    try testing.expect(st.isIgnored(\"debug.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:599:    try testing.expect(!st.isIgnored(\"other.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:607:    try parse(&arena, &out, \"\", \"[ab].log\\n\");\ncoding/gitignore.zig:613:    try testing.expect(st.isIgnored(\"a.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:614:    try testing.expect(st.isIgnored(\"b.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:615:    try testing.expect(!st.isIgnored(\"c.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:635:        try f.writeStreamingAll(io, \"*.log\\nbuild/\\n\");\ncoding/gitignore.zig:640:        try f.writeStreamingAll(io, \"!keep.log\\n\");\ncoding/gitignore.zig:647:    try testing.expect(st.isIgnored(\"foo.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:649:    try testing.expect(st.isIgnored(\"sub/foo.log\", false));\ncoding/gitignore.zig:650:    try testing.expect(!st.isIgnored(\"sub/keep.log\", false));\ncoding/mod.zig:49:    pub const catalog = @import(\"extensions_builtin/catalog.zig\");\ncoding/mod.zig:92:    _ = extensions_builtin.catalog;\ncoding/models_render.zig:1://! Models catalog renderer — §H.3 shape.\ncoding/models_render.zig:5://! gen-models` target) to write a regenerated catalog. Pure logic;\n"},{"role":"tool","tool_call_id":"call_855ewbsf","content":"[file_not_found] file does not exist"},{"role":"tool","tool_call_id":"call_4vakrvsl","content":"     1\t//! Leveled logging — §G.5 of the spec.\n     2\t//!\n     3\t//! A small, stderr-only, process-wide logger with a threshold set once\n     4\t//! at startup (and mutable at runtime via `setLevel`). Below the\n     5\t//! threshold, calls return early before any allocation — the\n     6\t//! `enabled(level)` check is the only thing a disabled call pays for.\n     7\t//!\n     8\t//! Levels (ascending verbosity):\n     9\t//!   - `err`   — unrecoverable errors above the event layer\n    10\t//!   - `warn`  — recoverable anomalies\n    11\t//!   - `info`  — default operator-useful output (e.g., resolved config)\n    12\t//!   - `debug` — step-level progress: HTTP round-trips, turn boundaries\n    13\t//!   - `trace` — full wire dumps: request/response bodies, SSE event\n    14\t//!               kinds, every message and tool result. May include\n    15\t//!               prompt content but **never** credential values.\n    16\t//!\n    17\t//! Scopes are an open enum by string — pass any short tag that groups\n    18\t//! related lines (e.g., `\"cfg\"`, `\"session\"`, `\"tool\"`, `\"http\"`,\n    19\t//! `\"message\"`, `\"turn\"`). The formatter just renders them verbatim.\n    20\t//!\n    21\t//! Output format:\n    22\t//!\n    23\t//!     {ms} {LEVEL} {scope} {event} key=value …\n    24\t//!\n    25\t//! where `ms` is milliseconds since `init` — short enough to read in a\n    26\t//! terminal, and ordered monotonically within one process run.\n    27\t\n    28\tconst std = @import(\"std\");\n    29\tconst stream_mod = @import(\"stream.zig\");\n    30\t\n    31\t/// Milliseconds since epoch. Delegates to `stream.nowMillis` which\n    32\t/// already handles the 0.17-dev API differences.\n    33\tfn nowMs() i64 {\n    34\t    return stream_mod.nowMillis();\n    35\t}\n    36\t\n    37\tpub const Level = enum(u8) {\n    38\t    err = 0,\n    39\t    warn = 1,\n    40\t    info = 2,\n    41\t    debug = 3,\n    42\t    trace = 4,\n    43\t\n    44\t    pub fn name(self: Level) []const u8 {\n    45\t        return switch (self) {\n    46\t            .err => \"ERROR\",\n    47\t            .warn => \"WARN \",\n    48\t            .info => \"INFO \",\n    49\t            .debug => \"DEBUG\",\n    50\t            .trace => \"TRACE\",\n    51\t        };\n    52\t    }\n    53\t\n    54\t    /// Parse a level tag (case-insensitive). Accepts both \"err\" and \"error\"\n    55\t    /// for ergonomics with `FRANKY_LOG=error`.\n    56\t    pub fn fromString(s: []const u8) ?Level {\n    57\t        var buf: [8]u8 = undefined;\n    58\t        if (s.len >= buf.len) return null;\n    59\t        for (s, 0..) |c, i| buf[i] = std.ascii.toLower(c);\n    60\t        const lower = buf[0..s.len];\n    61\t        if (std.mem.eql(u8, lower, \"err\") or std.mem.eql(u8, lower, \"error\")) return .err;\n    62\t        if (std.mem.eql(u8, lower, \"warn\") or std.mem.eql(u8, lower, \"warning\")) return .warn;\n    63\t        if (std.mem.eql(u8, lower, \"info\")) return .info;\n    64\t        if (std.mem.eql(u8, lower, \"debug\")) return .debug;\n    65\t        if (std.mem.eql(u8, lower, \"trace\")) return .trace;\n    66\t        return null;\n    67\t    }\n    68\t};\n    69\t\n    70\t/// Process-wide logger state. Default: silent (no sink wired — calls to\n    71\t/// `log()` no-op until `init()` runs). Writes to stderr are not\n    72\t/// serialized; log ordering across threads is best-effort. This is\n    73\t/// deliberate — grabbing a lock on every `log()` call would make the\n    74\t/// disabled fast path slower and, for a debug-only surface, occasional\n    75\t/// interleaving is preferable to a new global contention point.\n    76\tvar state_io: ?std.Io = null;\n    77\tvar state_level = std.atomic.Value(u8).init(@intFromEnum(Level.warn));\n    78\tvar state_started_ms: i64 = 0;\n    79\t/// v1.13.0 — when non-null, log/body write here instead of\n    80\t/// stderr. Set by `initWithFile`; reset to null by `init`.\n    81\t/// Owned by the logger; closed on `deinit` or `init`.\n    82\tvar state_sink_file: ?std.Io.File = null;\n    83\t\n    84\tpub fn init(io: std.Io, level: Level) void {\n    85\t    closeSinkFile();\n    86\t    state_io = io;\n    87\t    state_level.store(@intFromEnum(level), .seq_cst);\n    88\t    state_started_ms = nowMs();\n    89\t}\n    90\t\n    91\t/// v1.13.0 — like `init` but routes log output to `path` instead\n    92\t/// of stderr. Truncates on open (per-process log; not append).\n    93\t/// Parent dir is created best-effort. Returns `error.LogFileOpenFailed`\n    94\t/// when the file can't be created — callers in interactive mode\n    95\t/// catch and fall back to stderr with a stderr-banner so the TUI\n    96\t/// still starts (garbled, but functional).\n    97\tpub fn initWithFile(io: std.Io, level: Level, path: []const u8) !void {\n    98\t    init(io, level); // resets state_sink_file = null\n    99\t    if (std.fs.path.dirname(path)) |dir| {\n   100\t        std.Io.Dir.cwd().createDirPath(io, dir) catch {};\n   101\t    }\n   102\t    const f = std.Io.Dir.cwd().createFile(io, path, .{ .truncate = true }) catch\n   103\t        return error.LogFileOpenFailed;\n   104\t    state_sink_file = f;\n   105\t}\n   106\t\n   107\tpub fn deinit() void {\n   108\t    closeSinkFile();\n   109\t    state_io = null;\n   110\t}\n   111\t\n   112\tfn closeSinkFile() void {\n   113\t    if (state_sink_file) |f| {\n   114\t        if (state_io) |io| {\n   115\t            // Flush to disk before close so a separate openFile +\n   116\t            // read sees the bytes — important on macOS where the\n   117\t            // path resolves through a symlink (`/tmp` → `/private/tmp`)\n   118\t            // and the read-after-write timing can otherwise miss\n   119\t            // the trailing portion of the buffered output.\n   120\t            f.sync(io) catch {};\n   121\t            f.close(io);\n   122\t        }\n   123\t        state_sink_file = null;\n   124\t    }\n   125\t}\n   126\t\n   127\tfn sinkFile() std.Io.File {\n   128\t    return state_sink_file orelse std.Io.File.stderr();\n   129\t}\n   130\t\n   131\tpub fn setLevel(level: Level) void {\n   132\t    state_level.store(@intFromEnum(level), .seq_cst);\n   133\t}\n   134\t\n   135\tpub fn currentLevel() Level {\n   136\t    return @enumFromInt(state_level.load(.seq_cst));\n   137\t}\n   138\t\n   139\tpub fn enabled(level: Level) bool {\n   140\t    return @intFromEnum(level) <= state_level.load(.seq_cst) and state_io != null;\n   141\t}\n   142\t\n   143\t/// Emit one line: `{ms} {LEVEL} {scope} {event} {fmt-with-args}\\n`.\n   144\t///\n   145\t/// `fmt` is a Zig format string (may be empty). Returns silently on any\n   146\t/// I/O failure — logging should never crash the program.\n   147\tpub fn log(\n   148\t    level: Level,\n   149\t    scope: []const u8,\n   150\t    event: []const u8,\n   151\t    comptime fmt: []const u8,\n   152\t    args: anytype,\n   153\t) void {\n   154\t    if (!enabled(level)) return;\n   155\t    const io = state_io orelse return;\n   156\t\n   157\t    // Format the whole line into a local buffer, then push via\n   158\t    // `writeStreamingAll` — which respects the kernel-tracked\n   159\t    // file position so successive log/body calls append cleanly.\n   160\t    // (A fresh `File.Writer` per call starts at offset 0 and\n   161\t    // would overwrite previous writes.)\n   162\t    var buf: [2048]u8 = undefined;\n   163\t    var w: std.Io.Writer = .fixed(&buf);\n   164\t    const ms_since_start = nowMs() - state_started_ms;\n   165\t    w.print(\"{d: >6} {s} {s} {s}\", .{ ms_since_start, level.name(), scope, event }) catch return;\n   166\t    if (fmt.len > 0) {\n   167\t        w.writeByte(' ') catch return;\n   168\t        w.print(fmt, args) catch return;\n   169\t    }\n   170\t    w.writeByte('\\n') catch return;\n   171\t    sinkFile().writeStreamingAll(io, w.buffered()) catch return;\n   172\t}\n   173\t\n   174\t/// Dump a body (request/response/message text) at the given level.\n   175\t/// Truncates to `max_bytes`, prefixes with a header line and a trailer\n   176\t/// line so logs stay scannable. Newlines in `bytes` are preserved.\n   177\t///\n   178\t/// Use for trace-level wire dumps; at debug level and below, prefer\n   179\t/// `log(.debug, …, \"body_bytes={d}\", .{len})`.\n   180\tpub fn body(\n   181\t    level: Level,\n   182\t    scope: []const u8,\n   183\t    label: []const u8,\n   184\t    bytes: []const u8,\n   185\t    max_bytes: usize,\n   186\t) void {\n   187\t    if (!enabled(level)) return;\n   188\t    const io = state_io orelse return;\n   189\t\n   190\t    var hdr_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined;\n   191\t    var w: std.Io.Writer = .fixed(&hdr_buf);\n   192\t    const ms_since_start = nowMs() - state_started_ms;\n   193\t    const take = @min(bytes.len, max_bytes);\n   194\t    const truncated = take < bytes.len;\n   195\t    w.print(\n   196\t        \"{d: >6} {s} {s} body label={s} bytes={d}{s}\\n--- begin {s} ---\\n\",\n   197\t        .{ ms_since_start, level.name(), scope, label, bytes.len, if (truncated) \" (truncated)\" else \"\", label },\n   198\t    ) catch return;\n   199\t    const f = sinkFile();\n   200\t    f.writeStreamingAll(io, w.buffered()) catch return;\n   201\t    f.writeStreamingAll(io, bytes[0..take]) catch return;\n   202\t    if (truncated) f.writeStreamingAll(io, \"\\n… [truncated]\") catch return;\n   203\t    f.writeStreamingAll(io, \"\\n--- end \") catch return;\n   204\t    f.writeStreamingAll(io, label) catch return;\n   205\t    f.writeStreamingAll(io, \" ---\\n\") catch return;\n   206\t}\n   207\t\n   208\t// ─── tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   209\t\n   210\tconst testing = std.testing;\n   211\t\n   212\ttest \"Level.fromString accepts common spellings\" {\n   213\t    try testing.expectEqual(Level.err, Level.fromString(\"error\").?);\n   214\t    try testing.expectEqual(Level.err, Level.fromString(\"ERR\").?);\n   215\t    try testing.expectEqual(Level.warn, Level.fromString(\"Warning\").?);\n   216\t    try testing.expectEqual(Level.info, Level.fromString(\"INFO\").?);\n   217\t    try testing.expectEqual(Level.debug, Level.fromString(\"debug\").?);\n   218\t    try testing.expectEqual(Level.trace, Level.fromString(\"TRACE\").?);\n   219\t    try testing.expect(Level.fromString(\"nope\") == null);\n   220\t    try testing.expect(Level.fromString(\"\") == null);\n   221\t    try testing.expect(Level.fromString(\"loglevelthatistoolong\") == null);\n   222\t}\n   223\t\n   224\ttest \"enabled gates by threshold and initialization\" {\n   225\t    // Pristine state — not initialized.\n   226\t    deinit();\n   227\t    try testing.expect(!enabled(.err));\n   228\t    try testing.expect(!enabled(.trace));\n   229\t\n   230\t    // After init at .info, only .err/.warn/.info should pass.\n   231\t    var threaded = std.Io.Threaded.init(testing.allocator, .{\n   232\t        .argv0 = .empty,\n   233\t        .environ = .empty,\n   234\t    });\n   235\t    defer threaded.deinit();\n   236\t    init(threaded.io(), .info);\n   237\t    defer deinit();\n   238\t    try testing.expect(enabled(.err));\n   239\t    try testing.expect(enabled(.warn));\n   240\t    try testing.expect(enabled(.info));\n   241\t    try testing.expect(!enabled(.debug));\n   242\t    try testing.expect(!enabled(.trace));\n   243\t\n   244\t    setLevel(.trace);\n   245\t    try testing.expect(enabled(.trace));\n   246\t    try testing.expectEqual(Level.trace, currentLevel());\n   247\t}\n   248\t\n   249\ttest \"initWithFile: sets state_sink_file; subsequent init() resets it\" {\n   250\t    // Behavior under test is the sink-redirect *state machine* —\n   251\t    // we verify it directly via the module-private `state_sink_file`\n   252\t    // global rather than round-tripping through the filesystem.\n   253\t    // The latter was brittle across (Zig version × OS × tmpdir\n   254\t    // layout) without adding much over the integration coverage\n   255\t    // already exercised by interactive mode's auto-divert.\n   256\t    var threaded = std.Io.Threaded.init(testing.allocator, .{\n   257\t        .argv0 = .empty,\n   258\t        .environ = .empty,\n   259\t    });\n   260\t    defer threaded.deinit();\n   261\t    const io = threaded.io();\n   262\t\n   263\t    var tmp = std.testing.tmpDir(.{});\n   264\t    defer tmp.cleanup();\n   265\t    const path = try std.fmt.allocPrint(\n   266\t        testing.allocator,\n   267\t        \".zig-cache/tmp/{s}/franky.log\",\n   268\t        .{tmp.sub_path},\n   269\t    );\n   270\t    defer testing.allocator.free(path);\n   271\t\n   272\t    deinit(); // start clean\n   273\t    try testing.expect(state_sink_file == null);\n   274\t\n   275\t    try initWithFile(io, .info, path);\n   276\t    try testing.expect(state_sink_file != null);\n   277\t    // Logging is exercised here mostly to confirm it doesn't crash\n   278\t    // when the sink is a file; no read-back assertion.\n   279\t    log(.info, \"test\", \"hello\", \"key=value\", .{});\n   280\t    body(.info, \"test\", \"snippet\", \"BODY-CONTENT\", 64);\n   281\t\n   282\t    init(io, .info);\n   283\t    try testing.expect(state_sink_file == null);\n   284\t\n   285\t    deinit();\n   286\t}\n   287\t\n   288\ttest \"initWithFile: returns error.LogFileOpenFailed on un-creatable path\" {\n   289\t const io = threaded.io();\n   295\t    // `/dev/null/foo` works on both Linux and macOS as a path\n   296\t    // whose parent is a char device — `createFile` returns\n   297\t    // ENOTDIR which we     1\t//! Agent loop — §4.3-§4.5.\n     2\t//!\n     3\t//! `agentLoop` drives turns until: the assistant stops (no tool calls,\n     4\t//! no steering/follow-up) OR a tool sets `terminate = true` OR cancel\n     5\t//! fires. Each turn:\n     6\t//!   1. Call LLM via the registry.\n     7\t//!   2. Emit message_start/update/end for the assistant's streamed output.\n     8\t//!   3. For each tool call: beforeToolCall hook → execute → afterToolCall.\n     9\t//!   4. Emit toolResult messages in **source order**, append to history.\n    10\t//!   5. Check steering / follow-up hooks → append → loop.\n    11\t//!\n    12\t//! Invariants:\n    13\t//!   - All errors are stream events (`agent_error`), not raised.\n    14\t//!   - Tool `execute` may throw; the loop catches and wraps as `tool_runtime`.\n    15\t//!   - Callbacks must not throw; if they do, captured and emitted as\n    16\t//!     `internal`.\n    17\t\n    18\tconst std = @import(\"std\");\n    19\tconst ai = struct {\n    20\t    pub const types = @import(\"../ai/types.zig\");\n    21\t    pub const errors = @import(\"../ai/errors.zig\");\n    22\t    pub const stream = @import(\"../ai/stream.zig\");\n    23\t    pub const channel = @import(\"../ai/channel.zig\");\n    24\t    pub const registry = @import(\"../ai/registry.zig\");\n    25\t    pub const log = @import(\"../ai/log.zig\");\n    26\t};\n    27\tconst at = @import(\"types.zig\");\n    28\t\n    29\tpub const AgentChannel = ai.channel.Channel(at.AgentEvent);\n    30\t\n    31\t/// Extensible message type — for the MVP, agent messages ARE ai.types.Message.\n    32\t/// §4.2's \"AgentMessage superset\" is encoded via the `role == .custom` +\n    33\t/// `custom_role` fields, so custom roles can be filtered in `convertToLlm`.\n    34\tpub const AgentMessage = ai.types.Message;\n    35\t\n    36\tpub const ConvertToLlmFn = *const fn (\n    37\t    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    38\t    messages: []const AgentMessage,\n    39\t) anyerror![]ai.types.Message;\n    40\t\n    41\t/// Default convertToLlm: pass-through, with two custom-role rewrites:\n    42\t///   - `custom_role == \"compaction_summary\"` is converted to a plain\n    43\t///     `user` message whose first text block is prefixed with\n    44\t///     `\"Earlier in this conversation:\\n\\n\"`. The model sees this as\n    45\t///     ordinary context rather than a synthetic role it doesn't know.\n    46\t///   - Any other `.custom` role is filtered out (unknown to the model).\n    47\tpub fn defaultConvertToLlm(\n    48\t    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    49\t    messages: []const AgentMessage,\n    50\t) ![]ai.types.Message {\n\n[Showing lines 1-50 of 1568. Explicit CLI flag wins.\n   612\t    if (cfg.log_level) |s| {\n   613\t        if (ai.log.Level.fromString(s)) |l| return l;\n   614\t        // Unknown value: fall through rather than error. Log level is\n   615\t        // diagnostic — a typo shouldn't stop a run.\n   616\t    }\n   617\t    // 2. FRANKY_LOG env var.\n   618\t    if (environ.getPosix(\"FRANKY_LOG\")) |s| {\n   619\t        if (ai.log.Level.fromString(s)) |l| return l;\n   620\t    }\n   621\t    // 3. FRANKY_DEBUG=1 → debug.\n   622\t    if (environ.getPosix(\"FRANKY_DEBUG\")) |v| {\n   623\t        if (v.len > 0 and v[0] != '0') return .debug;\n   624\t    }\n   625\t    // 4. --verbose → info.\n   626\t    if (cfg.verbose) return .info;\n   627\t    // 5. Default: warnings and errors only.\n   628\t    return .warn;\n   629\t}\n   630\t\n   631\t/// Resolve `ai.registry.Timeouts` for this run.\n   632\t///\n   633\t/// Precedence per field: CLI flag → env var → autodetected default.\n   634\t///\n   635\t/// The autodetected default is normally `Timeouts{}` (the §G.4\n   636\t/// standard), but bumps `first_byte_ms` to 10 minutes when\n   637\t/// `cfg.base_url` points at a loopback host (Ollama on\n   638\t/// `localhost:11434`, LM-Studio on `localhost:1234`, vLLM on\n   639\t/// `localhost:8000`, …). Local LLMs commonly take longer than\n   640\t/// 30 s to emit a first token under reasoning workloads, and the\n   641\t/// hard cap surfaced as the misleading `transport: http error:\n   642\t/// Timeout` the user reported pre-v1.10. An explicit\n   643\t/// `--first-byte-timeout-ms` (or env var) still wins.\n   644\t///\n   645\t/// Map-based so every mode (print stays on the live `Environ`;\n\n[Showing lines 606-645 of 2268. .{@errorName(err)});\n"},{"role":"assistant","content":null,"tool_calls":[{"id":"call_9wb1zmqq","type":"function","function":{"name":"read","arguments":"{\"offset\":1145,\"path\":\"src/agent/loop.zig\",\"limit\":50}"}},{"id":"call_3r8uuepm","type":"function","function":{"name":"read","arguments":"{\"offset\":380,\"path\":\"src/agent/loop.zig\",\"limit\":20}"}}]},{"role":"tool","tool_call_id":"call_9wb1zmqq","content":"  1145\t/// Dump one message to the trace log. Text and tool-call args are\n  1146\t/// written verbatim (truncated to 4 KiB per block); binary and image\n  1147\t/// blocks are summarised. Caller must have already checked\n  1148\t/// `log.enabled(.trace)`.\n  1149\tfn logMessageTrace(direction: []const u8, index: usize, msg: ai.types.Message) void {\n  1150\t    const role_str: []const u8 = switch (msg.role) {\n  1151\t        .user => \"user\",\n  1152\t        .assistant => \"assistant\",\n  1153\t        .tool_result => \"tool_result\",\n  1154\t        .custom => \"custom\",\n  1155\t    };\n  1156\t    ai.log.log(.trace, \"message\", direction, \"idx={d} role={s} blocks={d}\", .{ index, role_str, msg.content.len });\n  1157\t    // Tool-result messages carry the sub-id and is_error on the Message,\n  1158\t    // not per content block — surface those up front.\n  1159\t    if (msg.role == .tool_result) {\n  1160\t        ai.log.log(.trace, \"message\", \"tool_result_meta\", \"tool_use_id={s} is_error={}\", .{\n  1161\t            msg.tool_call_id orelse \"\",\n  1162\t            msg.is_error,\n  1163\t        });\n  1164\t    }\n  1165\t    for (msg.content, 0..) |cb, bi| switch (cb) {\n  1166\t        .text => |t| ai.log.body(.trace, \"message\", \"text\", t.text, 4096),\n  1167\t        .thinking => |th| ai.log.body(.trace, \"message\", \"thinking\", th.thinking, 4096),\n  1168\t        .image => ai.log.log(.trace, \"message\", \"image\", \"block={d}\", .{bi}),\n  1169\t        .tool_call => |tc| {\n  1170\t            ai.log.log(.trace, \"message\", \"tool_call\", \"block={d} id={s} name={s}\", .{ bi, tc.id, tc.name });\n  1171\t            ai.log.body(.trace, \"message\", \"tool_args\", tc.arguments_json, 4096);\n  1172\t        },\n  1173\t    };\n  1174\t}\n  1175\t\n  1176\tfn pushAgentError(\n  1177\t    out: *AgentChannel,\n  1178\t    io: std.Io,\n  1179\t    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n  1180\t    code: ai.errors.Code,\n  1181\t    message: []const u8,\n  1182\t) !void {\n  1183\t    const owned = try allocator.dupe(u8, message);\n  1184\t    out.closeWithFinal(io, .{ .agent_error = .{ .code = code, .message = owned } });\n  1185\t}\n  1186\t\n  1187\t/// vN — emit the graceful interrupt event and close the channel.\n  1188\tfn emitInterrupted(\n  1189\t    out: *AgentChannel,\n  1190\t    io: std.Io,\n  1191\t    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n  1192\t) !void {\n  1193\t    _ = allocator;\n  1194\t    ai.log.log(.info, \"loop\", \"interrupted\", \"graceful stop after current turn\", .{});\n\n[Showing lines 1145-1194 of 1568. Use offset=1195 to continue.]\n"},{"role":"tool","tool_call_id":"call_3r8uuepm","content":"   380\t    config: Config,\n   381\t    out: *AgentChannel,\n   382\t) !bool {\n   383\t    try out.push(io, .turn_start);\n   384\t    ai.log.log(.debug, \"turn\", \"start\", \"messages_in_transcript={d}\", .{transcript.messages.items.len});\n   385\t\n   386\t    // Build context from messages.\n   387\t    const llm_messages = try config.convert_to_llm(allocator, transcript.messages.items);\n   388\t    if (ai.log.enabled(.trace)) {\n   389\t        for (llm_messages, 0..) |m, i| {\n   390\t            logMessageTrace(\"send\", i, m);\n   391\t        }\n   392\t    }\n   393\t    // Clone tools into the Context tools slice.\n   394\t    const tools_ctx = try cloneTools(allocator, config.tools);\n   395\t    const context: ai.types.Context = .{\n   396\t        .system_prompt = try allocator.dupe(u8, config.system_prompt),\n   397\t        .messages = llm_messages,\n   398\t        .tools = tools_ctx,\n   399\t    };\n\n[Showing lines 380-399 of 1568.
